minimal.label: Minimal Anxiety
minimal.recommendation: Preventive CBT techniques and stress management

mild.label: Mild Anxiety
mild.recommendation: Self-help CBT strategies and lifestyle modifications

questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you felt nervous, anxious, or on edge?
questions.text_field_prompt: Describe what triggers this nervous feeling and where you feel it in your body:

questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been unable to stop or control worrying?
questions.text_field_prompt: What topics do you worry about most? What have you tried to stop the worry?

questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you worried too much about different things?
questions.text_field_prompt: List the main categories of things you worry about (health, work, relationships, etc.):

questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you had trouble relaxing?
questions.text_field_prompt: What prevents you from relaxing? What thoughts run through your mind when you try to relax?

questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been so restless that it is hard to sit still?
questions.text_field_prompt: Describe your restlessness. What do you do when you feel this way?

questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you become easily annoyed or irritable?
questions.text_field_prompt: What situations trigger your irritability? How do you typically respond?

questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you felt afraid as if something awful might happen?
questions.text_field_prompt: What specific awful things do you fear? How likely do you think they actually are?

questions.question: How often do you worry about things that are unlikely to happen?
questions.text_field_prompt: Give examples of unlikely events you worry about. What makes these fears feel real?

questions.question: How often do you seek reassurance from others to reduce your anxiety?
questions.text_field_prompt: Who do you seek reassurance from? What do you ask them? Does it help long-term?

questions.question: How often do you engage in 'what if' thinking about future problems?
questions.text_field_prompt: List your most common 'what if' thoughts. How far into the future do they go?

questions.question: How often do you feel you must worry to prevent bad things from happening?
questions.text_field_prompt: What bad things do you believe worrying prevents? Is there evidence for this belief?

questions.question: How often do you have difficulty tolerating uncertainty about the future?
questions.text_field_prompt: What uncertainties are hardest to tolerate? What do you do to try to eliminate uncertainty?

questions.question: How often do you mentally rehearse conversations or scenarios to prepare for them?
questions.text_field_prompt: What situations do you rehearse? Does this preparation actually help or increase anxiety?
questions.cbt_focus: Mental rehearsal and worry as preparation

questions.question: How often do you worry about your health or physical symptoms?
questions.text_field_prompt: What health concerns do you have? How often do you check your body or research symptoms?

questions.question: How often do you worry about finances or job security?
questions.text_field_prompt: What specific financial or work worries do you have? What would resolve these concerns?

questions.question: How often do you worry about the safety of loved ones?
questions.text_field_prompt: Who do you worry about most? What specific dangers do you imagine?
questions.cbt_focus: Attachment anxiety and realistic risk assessment

questions.question: How often do you experience rapid heartbeat or heart palpitations?
questions.text_field_prompt: When does your heart race? What thoughts accompany this sensation?

questions.question: How often do you experience shortness of breath or feeling smothered?
questions.text_field_prompt: Describe these breathing episodes. What do you fear is happening when they occur?

questions.question: How often do you experience dizziness or lightheadedness?
questions.text_field_prompt: When do you feel dizzy? Do you avoid situations where this might happen?

questions.question: How often do you experience muscle tension or tightness?
questions.text_field_prompt: Where in your body do you hold tension? When is it worst?

questions.question: How often do you experience sweating or hot flashes when anxious?
questions.text_field_prompt: In what situations does this occur? How does it affect your behavior?

questions.question: How often do you experience nausea or stomach upset?
questions.text_field_prompt: When does your stomach bother you? Do you avoid eating or certain foods because of this?

questions.question: How often do you experience trembling or shaking?
questions.text_field_prompt: When do you notice shaking? What do you fear others will think if they notice?

questions.question: How often do you feel detached from yourself or your surroundings (derealization)?
questions.text_field_prompt: Describe these experiences. What triggers them? How do you respond?

questions.question: How often do you experience chest pain or tightness?
questions.text_field_prompt: Have you had medical evaluation for chest pain? What do you fear when this happens?

questions.question: How often do you avoid social situations due to anxiety?
questions.text_field_prompt: What social situations do you avoid? What do you fear will happen if you attend?

questions.question: How often do you avoid places or situations where escape might be difficult?
questions.text_field_prompt: List places you avoid (crowds, public transport, etc.). What is your specific fear?

questions.question: How often do you avoid physical exercise fearing it will trigger anxiety symptoms?
questions.text_field_prompt: What exercise do you avoid? What symptoms do you fear will occur?

questions.question: How often do you avoid watching news or distressing media content?
questions.text_field_prompt: What specific content triggers your anxiety? Do you completely avoid it or limit exposure?

questions.question: How often do you avoid making decisions to prevent anxiety?
questions.text_field_prompt: What types of decisions are hardest? What do you fear will happen if you choose wrong?

questions.question: How often do you avoid being alone because anxiety increases?
questions.text_field_prompt: What thoughts or fears arise when you're alone? Who do you seek out for company?

questions.question: How often do you avoid traveling far from home?
questions.text_field_prompt: How far from home feels safe? What do you fear will happen if you travel further?

questions.question: How often do you avoid confrontations or difficult conversations?
questions.text_field_prompt: What conversations do you avoid? What catastrophic outcome do you imagine?

questions.question: How often do you avoid trying new things due to fear of failure?
questions.text_field_prompt: What new experiences do you avoid? What does failure mean to you personally?
questions.cbt_focus: Fear of failure and behavioral experiments

questions.question: How often do you check your body for signs of illness or problems?
questions.text_field_prompt: What do you check (pulse, lumps, breathing)? How many times per day?

questions.question: How often do you carry safety items (water, medication, phone) 'just in case'?
questions.text_field_prompt: What items do you carry? What do you fear would happen without them?

questions.question: How often do you research symptoms or health information online?
questions.text_field_prompt: How many hours per week do you spend researching? Does it reduce or increase your anxiety?

questions.question: How often do you have escape plans or know exits when entering buildings?
questions.text_field_prompt: When did you start noticing exits? What do you fear would happen without an escape route?

questions.question: How often do you ask others to accompany you to anxiety-provoking situations?
questions.text_field_prompt: Who do you rely on? What do you believe they prevent from happening?

questions.question: How often do you practice breathing or relaxation techniques to prevent anxiety?
questions.text_field_prompt: When do you use these techniques? Are they preventative or responsive?
questions.cbt_focus: Relaxation as safety behavior vs. skill

questions.question: How often do you rehearse what you'll say before speaking in groups?
questions.text_field_prompt: What do you rehearse? Does preparation help or increase pressure?

questions.question: How often do you check and recheck work or tasks for errors?
questions.text_field_prompt: How many times do you check? What errors do you fear making?
questions.cbt_focus: Checking compulsions and intolerance of uncertainty

questions.question: How often do you avoid caffeine or other substances that might increase anxiety?
questions.text_field_prompt: What substances do you avoid? What physical sensations do you fear?

questions.question: How much do your anxiety symptoms interfere with your work or school performance?
questions.text_field_prompt: Describe specific ways anxiety affects your productivity or attendance:

questions.question: How much do your anxiety symptoms interfere with your social life?
questions.text_field_prompt: What social activities have you given up? How has this affected relationships?

questions.question: How much do your anxiety symptoms interfere with your family responsibilities?
questions.text_field_prompt: What family duties are affected? How do family members respond to your anxiety?

questions.question: How often do you experience panic attacks (sudden intense fear with physical symptoms)?
questions.text_field_prompt: Describe your most recent panic attack. What triggered it? How long did it last?

questions.question: How much time per day do you spend worrying or feeling anxious?
questions.text_field_prompt: When during the day is worry worst? What activities are disrupted by worry time?

questions.question: How often do you feel in control of your anxiety?
questions.text_field_prompt: What strategies help you feel in control? When do you feel most helpless?

questions.question: How hopeful are you that your anxiety can be effectively managed?
questions.text_field_prompt: What would give you more hope? What previous attempts have succeeded or failed?
